description: Fewer returns than the minimum window required for a reliable classification; expect None/'unknown' (graceful degradation, not an error).

inputs.description: Only 3 observations. AC and vol estimates are unreliable at N<some minimum (e.g. N<20). Must degrade gracefully, never raise.

derivation.minimum_observations: Any AC/vol-based classifier needs at minimum enough observations to compute a meaningful rolling autocorrelation. 3 observations yield only 2 lag-1 pairs; this is statistically meaningless. The function must return None or a sentinel 'unknown' string rather than produce a spurious label.
derivation.expected_label: None or 'unknown' — the specific sentinel depends on implementation choice, but the test asserts is_none_or_unknown(result) so either is valid. Fixture records None as the canonical expectation; test code asserts the actual result is falsy OR == 'unknown'.
derivation.tolerance_note: No float comparison needed. Test checks: result is None or result == 'unknown'.
